mission.title: argo: Maintain difference in cluster and git values for specific fields
mission.description: Maintain difference in cluster and git values for specific fields. Requested by 72+ users.

steps.title: Check current argo deployment
steps.description:
Verify your argo version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo
helm list -n argo 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working argo installation.

steps.title: Review argo configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo
kubectl get configmap -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo
```
If another controller modifies a field in a resource and that field is required by the resource to be created, then that resource cannot be compatible with GitOps.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Maintain difference in cluster and git values for specific
steps.description:
Leveraging `ignoreDifferences` may sound a good direction to go but there are some downsides that could make ArgoCD `Application` management a bit more cumbersome. A few things to consider about this design:

- Setting up every field to be ignored in the `Application` is a reactive process as
```yaml
spec:
  ignoreDifferences:
  - group: apps
    kind: Deployment
    jsonPointers:
    - /spec/replicas
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo
kubectl get events -n argo --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Maintain difference in cluster and git values for specific" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Leveraging `ignoreDifferences` may sound a good direction to go but there are some downsides that could make ArgoCD `Application` management a bit more cumbersome. A few things to consider about this design:

- Setting up every field to be ignored in the `Application` is a reactive process as different controllers/users will be modifying fields in the same resource.
codeSnippets:
spec:
  ignoreDifferences:
  - group: apps
    kind: Deployment
    jsonPointers:
    - /spec/replicas

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: nginx
  labels:
    app: nginx
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: nginx
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: nginx
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: nginx
        image: nginx:latest
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo installed or the issue environment reproducible.
